title: FINS3666 Lecture Slides Week 10 Danny
body:
FINS3666: Trading & Market Making
Lecture 10: Advanced Topic (Setting up a Fund)




                     CHEN YU (DANNY) YAN, PhD
                                  2025 Term 1
  Guest Speaker: Chen Yu (Danny) Yan, PhD
    Danny Yan has worked in Trading and Market Making since 2014.

    Senior Execution Consultant at Cboe Group

    Quantitative Trader at Boronia Capital

    Risk and Dealing Manager at FP Markets

    Proprietary Trader at State One Capital & Aliom Trading




Magic       Snowboarding       Spearfishing       Chess
    Agenda – Advanced
Topics (Setting up a Fund)

1. Types of trading business
2. Why do you need a fund?
3. Types of funds
4. Finding your edge
5. Strategy generation
6. Infrastructure setup
7. Understanding the law
8. Finding your team
                 Types of Trading Business
Proprietary Trading • High leverage with focus on diversified small portfolios /
                      traders
      House         • Trades using own capital


                              • Profits from the spread and rebates for providing liquidity
   Market Makers              • Focus on minimising holding periods



    Commodity                 • Logistic and storage facilities (supply chain based)
    Trading Firm              • Trades both physical and financial products


                              • Mostly require external capital
         Funds                • Income based on performance and management fees
                              • Large variety of trading strategies
 Why do you need a Fund ?
                Tax
              Benefits
             Access to
               capital
           Can’t find a job
         Legal & Regulatory
              protection
        Don’t want to work for
            someone else
   Strategy scaling / Infrastructure
                needs
       Compensation structure
 (Management & Performance Fees)
                 Types of Funds
Mutual Fund

Exchange Traded Fund (ETFs)

Private Equity Fund

Hedge Fund
                     Finding Your Edge
What makes your strategy unique ?
§ Product
§ Strategy
§ Infrastructure
How are you making your money ?
§ Market making illiquid markets
§ Modelling complex products
§ Unique arbitrage strategies
§ Insider trading
                Finding Your Edge: Uniqueness
§ All elements of uniqueness arise from information advantage
§ If you know something that nobody else in the market does, then
  that is your edge.

          Product                Strategy              Infrastructure
     § Trading a new        § Market information      § Being faster
       product or a new       advantage               § More robust
       combination of       § Arbitrage or
       products               statistical arbitrage
     § Designing your own     opportunities
       product
 Finding Your Edge: How to Make Money (1)




      Market   Complex      Unique
      Making   Modelling   Arbitrage
        .          .           .
      Finding Your Edge: How to Make Money (2)
Market Making
§ A noble way of making your
  money. You provide liquidity for
  illiquid markets and reduce
  friction for people who wishes to
  invest
§ You are rewarded by the market
  for taking the risk of reducing
  friction
§ Often requires expensive
  infrastructure
§ Almost never loses.
       Finding Your Edge: How to Make Money (3)
Complex modelling
"I can calculate the motions of heavenly
bodies, but not the madness of people“ – Sir
Isaac Newton


§ Pros: You can make a lot of
  money
§ Cons: When you fail to make a lot
  of money, it means that you’re not
  as smart as you think
      Finding Your Edge: How to Make Money (4)
Unique Arbitrage
§ A strategy that almost entirely
  relies on an information edge. If
  you can’t keep a secret, consider
  another strategy
§ Assumed to be mythical by most
  financial academics.
               Strategy Generation



Manual vs     Trading
                        Scalability   Costs   Checklist!
Algorithmic    Style
           Strategy Generation: The Checklist
1. Strategy Profitability and Downside Assessment
2. Know Your Strategy
3. Backend Codebase and Optimisation
4. Data Pipeline and Data Cleaning
5. Debugging !
6. Going Live
7. Risk Management and Optimisation
 Infrastructure Setup (1)



           vs.
 Infrastructure Setup (2)
                    § Cloud vs Static
  Data Connection     infrastructure
   & Computation    § Types of API (rest, FIX etc.)
                    § Data feeds

     Stability &    § Direct market access, order
                      routers etc.
       Speed        § Ping, jitter etc.


                    § Selecting prime broker(s)
      Broker        § Product specialisation
                           Infrastructure Setup (3)
§ Your talented team is your most fundamental (and expensive)
  infrastructure !
   Operating Costs (per annum)                                        Startup    Mature
   Staffing costs (trading, quant, technology + legal & compliance)   $$ x      $$$$ x
   Client Reporting and Portal
   Technology Costs
   § Data Management                                                   $20k       $5mm
   § Market Access & Servers                                            $5k       $2mm
   § Risk management (cybersecurity, position management)
   § Execution Engine
   § Backtesting Engine
                  Understanding the Law
§ Make sure your operation is legal!
  For example, Crypto is outlawed in
  China
§ Know your company and tax
  structures
§ Know the local policies
§ Beware of the source of funds,
  don’t fund terrorism
 Finding Your Team


                        Boring guy (legal and
               Trader
                        compliance/risk)
   Tech
   Guy




          Numbers Guy
 Congratulations, Now You Have a Fund
 David E. Shaw
